defects.name: Cigar End Rot
defects.description: Fungus causing dry rot at the flower tip. Affected area is blackened and resembles cigar ash.
defects.tolerance: Zero tolerance for Class I; Major defect if infection extends >10mm.
defects.action: Remove affected clusters immediately to prevent fungal spread.

defects.name: Bruising
defects.description: Sunken or flat patches on the peel that darken as fruit ripens.
defects.tolerance: Major if >6cm² per cluster; Minor if <6cm².
defects.action: Sort for immediate local ripening; do not use for long-distance transport.

defects.name: Anthracnose
defects.description: Large brown/black circular spots with salmon-pink spore masses in the center.
defects.tolerance: Scored as serious damage if reaching the pulp.
defects.action: Discard fruit; sanitize storage area to prevent cross-contamination.

defects.name: Anthracnose
defects.description: Small dark spots enlarging to irregular black smears, often starting from the stem.
defects.tolerance: Serious damage if affecting >5% of surface or slightly sunken.
defects.action: Sort for local immediate sale; prioritize high-turnover retail.

defects.name: Sap Burn
defects.description: Chemical burn from latex causing skin streaking and necrosis.
defects.tolerance: Major if >15% of surface area is affected.
defects.action: Downgrade to Class II; ensure fruit is washed to stop further burning.

defects.name: Stem-End Rot
defects.description: Fungal infection at the stem causing translucent brown flesh and decay.
defects.tolerance: Zero tolerance; leads to total internal decay.
defects.action: Discard immediately; identify harvest source to review sterilization protocols.

defects.name: Chilling Injury
defects.description: Sunken black lesions or vascular browning from improper cooling.
defects.tolerance: Serious damage if affecting internal quality or taste.
defects.action: Audit cold chain logs; check storage temperatures (avoid <13°C for early season).
